subject: General Aptitude
question:
A class of twelve children has two more boys than girls. A group of three children are randomly picked from this class to accompany the teacher on a field trip. What is the probability that the group accompanying the teacher contains more girls than boys?

A. 6/864
B. 5/12
C. 1/12
D. 625/864
answer: The official answer key marks this question as excluded (X). Computing: class has 7 boys and 5 girls. Groups with more girls than boys: 3 girls 0 boys or 2 girls 1 boy. P = [C(5,3) + C(5,2)*C(7,1)] / C(12,3) = [10 + 70] / 220 = 80/220 = 4/11. The answer key X indicates the question was excluded from official scoring.

parserGuess.stem: A class of twelve children has two more boys than girls. A group of three children are randomly picked from this class to accompany the teacher on a field trip. What is the probability that the group accompanying the teacher contains more girls than boys?

subject: General Aptitude
question:
Details of prices of two items P and Q are presented in the above table. The ratio of cost of item P to cost of item Q is 3:4. Discount is calculated as the difference between the marked price and the selling price. The profit percentage is calculated as the ratio of the difference between selling price and cost to the cost, multiplied by 100.

Table:
Items | Cost (r) | Profit % | Marked Price (r)
P     |    --    |    --    |     5,800
Q     |    25    |    --    |    10,000

The discount on item Q, as a percentage of its marked price, is ____

A. B. 12.5    C. 25    D. 5

subject: Discrete Mathematics
question: State whether the following statement is TRUE or FALSE: There is a linear-time algorithm for testing the planarity of finite graphs.

subject: General Aptitude
question:
Four equilateral triangles are used to form a regular closed three-dimensional object by joining along the edges. The angle between any two faces is:

A. 30°
B. 60°
C. 45°
D. 90°
answer: X. The correct dihedral angle of a regular tetrahedron is arccos(1/3) ≈ 70.53°, which does not match any listed option; the question is excluded from scoring.

parserGuess.stem: Four equilateral triangles are used to form a regular closed three-dimensional object by joining along the edges. The angle between any two faces is:

subject: General Aptitude
question:
The Canadian constitution requires that equal importance be given to English and French. Last year, Air Canada lost a lawsuit, and had to pay a six-figure fine to a French-speaking couple after they filed complaints about formal in-flight announcements in English lasting 13 seconds, as opposed to informal 5 second messages in French.

The French-speaking couple filed a complaint against Air Canada because

A. the in-flight announcements being made in English
B. the English announcements being longer than the French ones
C. the English announcements being longer than the French ones
D. equal importance being given to both English and French
E. equal importance being given to both English and French

Based on the given passage, which one of the following is the correct inference?

A. the in-flight announcements being made in English
B. the English announcements being longer than the French ones
C. the English announcements being longer than the French ones
D. equal importance being given to both English and French
answer: C. the English announcements being longer than the French ones

options.text: the in-flight announcements being made in English
the English announcements being longer than the French ones
the English announcements being longer than the French ones

subject: Discrete Mathematics
question: Show that proposition C is a logical consequence of the formula A and (A -> (B or C)) and (B -> not A) using truth tables.
answer: C is a logical consequence: in every interpretation that makes A and (A -> (B or C)) and (B -> not A) True, C is also True.

subject: Discrete Mathematics
question: Every subset of a countable set is countable. State whether the above statement is true or false with reason.
answer: True. Every subset of a countable set is countable.

subject: Discrete Mathematics
question: Match the pairs in the following. (a) Groups  (b) Semigroups  (c) Monoids  (d) Abelian groups   with   (p) Associativity  (q) Identity  (r) Commutativity  (s) Left inverse
answer: a-s, b-p, c-q, d-r  (Groups - Left inverse, Semigroups - Associativity, Monoids - Identity, Abelian groups - Commutativity)

parserGuess.stem: Match the pairs in the following.

options.text: Abelian groups with (p) Associativity (q) Identity (r) Commutativity (s) Left inverse

subject: Discrete Mathematics
question:
Let L be a set with a relation R which is transitive, anti-symmetric and reflexive and for any two elements a, b ∈ L, let the least upper bound lub(a,b) and the greatest lower bound glb(a,b) exist. Which of the following is true?
A. L is a poset
B. L is a Boolean algebra
C. L is a lattice
D. None of the above

subject: Algorithms
question:
For the undirected, weighted graph given below, which of the following sequences of edges represents a correct execution of Prim's algorithm to construct a Minimum Spanning Tree?

(Graph: nodes b, c, d, e, f, g, h with edges and weights:
b-c: 5, b-e: 2, b-f: 3, c-d: 4, c-e: 2, d-e: 2, d-h: 6, e-f: 1, e-g: 4, f-g: 3, g-h: 1)

A. (a,b), (d,f), (f,c), (g,i), (d,h), (a,h), (e,h)
B. (h,g), (g,f), (f,e), (e,b), (b,c), (g,b), (h,f), (f,e)
C. (a,b), (f,c), (c,d), (d,h), (b,e), (f,e), (d,g), (h,b), (f,e)
D. (b,g), (g,i), (h,f), (f,c), (d,h), (d,e), (d,a), (h,b), (f,c)

(From image: options represent edge sequences for MST construction starting from a vertex)

A. (a, b), (d, f), (f, c), (g, i), (d, h), (a, h), (e, h)
B. (a, b), (a, f), (f, e), (e, c), (e, d), (d, h)
C. (a, b), (f, c), (f, e), (e, c), (d, e), (d, h)
D. (a, b), (f, c), (c, e), (e, d), (d, h), (g, e)

subject: Algorithms
question:
A language with string manipulation facilities uses the following operations:
  head(s): first character of a string
  tail(s): all but the first character of a string
  concat(s1, s2): concatenation of s1 and s2

For the string s = "abcd", what will be the output of concat(head(s), head(tail(tail(s))))?
